title: Files (Q&A)
description: Understanding file types, cuts, safety, and filing techniques.

questions.prompt: Match the correct file name to its cross-section image.
Describe the difference between single and double cut files.
questions.exampleContext: Single cut files have teeth running in one direction, leaving a smoother finish. Double cut files have two sets of teeth crossing each other, removing material faster.

questions.prompt: Review the image of the file. List three checks you would make to ensure it is safe to use.
questions.exampleContext: Before using a file, you should check that the handle is secure, the teeth are clean (free of pinned material), and there is no obvious damage to the tool.

questions.prompt: Describe how you would prepare material to be filed.
questions.exampleContext: Preparing material involves cleaning the surface, marking out where you need to file, and securing it firmly in a vice.

questions.prompt: Explain what soft jaws / vice jaw plates are used for and the consequences of not using them.
questions.exampleContext: Soft jaws (vice jaw plates) protect the workpiece from being damaged or marked by the hard, serrated jaws of the vice.

questions.prompt: State the tool and technique required to achieve a flat surface.
questions.exampleContext: To achieve a flat surface, use a flat file and apply the cross-filing technique (moving the file diagonally across the workpiece).

questions.prompt: State the tool and technique required to achieve a smooth surface finish.
questions.exampleContext: To achieve a smooth surface finish, use the draw-filing technique, holding the file at both ends and pushing it sideways along the work.
